name: Tim Kaine
subject: An update on Attorney General Bill Barr
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | | **Add your name if you agree that Robert Mueller should testify publicly so that the American people can hear about his investigation:** | ******[**ADD MY NAME**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1749/3e0906/7bcad288/3e30b151/3680454621/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/robert-mueller-must-testify?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+ADD+MY+NAME&utm_campaign=20190501_1_PET_TK_Barr-Mueller&source=20190501_1_PET_TK_Barr-Mueller")******
---

Today Attorney General Bill Barr testified in front of the Senate Judiciary Committee about the Special Counsel's report -- and his handling of it.

Derek, let me just say this: If I were Robert Mueller, I'd be incredibly frustrated right now. Because I'll tell you what, I read the four-page summary that AG Barr released. And then I read the Mueller report. **And that four-page summary was a political spin job, plain and simple.** _It doesn 't even make an attempt to accurately characterize Bob Mueller's four-hundred-page report -- and the severe misconduct he found!_

**The American people deserve to hear directly from Robert Mueller about his conclusions. I believe Mueller should testify publicly -- after the blatant mischaracterizations by the Attorney General, it's critical that we set the record straight.** [**Sign my petition if you agree.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1749/3e0906/7bcad288/3e30b150/3680454621/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/robert-mueller-must-testify?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+Sign+my+petition+if+you+agree&utm_campaign=20190501_1_PET_TK_Barr-Mueller&source=20190501_1_PET_TK_Barr-Mueller")

Derek, Barr is acting more like Donald Trump's defense lawyer or a TV commentator rather than the Attorney General of the United States. Special Counsel Mueller himself said in a strongly-worded letter to Barr that his irresponsible summary led to completely misguided interpretations of the report.

As Mueller wrote, "_There is now public confusion about critical aspects of the results of our investigation."_ Well, here's how we can clear up the public confusion: Allow Robert Mueller to publicly testify about his results.

**Add your name if you agree:** [**Mueller must testify.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1749/3e0906/7bcad288/3e30b157/3680454621/VEsF/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/robert-mueller-must-testify?utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+Mueller+must+testify&utm_campaign=20190501_1_PET_TK_Barr-Mueller&source=20190501_1_PET_TK_Barr-Mueller")

Thanks,

_Tim_

Original Message from Kamala Harris, May 1, 2019

I just got done questioning Attorney General Bill Barr in the Judiciary
Committee. He was called to testify about Special Counsel Robert Mueller’s
investigation into Russian interference in our election in 2016 and his
subsequent summary of that investigation.

I’ll be frank: Attorney General Barr refused to answer most of my
questions -- and when he did, his answers were alarming. He admitted he
had never even reviewed the underlying evidence of the Mueller report, and
he refused to say whether the President has ever ordered him to
investigate anyone. This is unacceptable.

Bill Barr is acting more
like the President’s personal attorney than the Attorney General of the
United States. His job is to defend the rule of law and serve the American
people, not shield the President from justice. He made something very
clear today: he must resign.

I’m running for president because the American people deserve truth and
integrity from their elected leaders. That’s not what we’re getting right
now. If you’re with me in this fight, I need you now.

Team --
Recent Affordable Care Act headlines are incredibly alarming. Take this one, from the Washington Post: _" Trump administration asks court to completely invalidate Obama's Affordable Care Act."_
You're reading this correctly: **The Trump administration is trying to fully repeal the Affordable Care Act in its entirety in federal court.**
The stakes are extremely high, everyone, and we need all hands on deck.** [Will you please sign our petition calling on the Trump administration to stop attacking health care?](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2f9/2a380d31/38442a17/f12e92/1245523495/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/protect-the-ACA/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2019_protect-the-aca-up%3Frefcode%3De20190417_acaja_all")**
If Trump is successful, it could mean that people with pre-existing conditions will lose their badly-needed protections. But it's not just people with pre-existing conditions who would be vulnerable -- millions of Americans could lose their coverage altogether.
Look, Senator Hassan believes that everyone deserves access to the care they need to get and stay healthy. And, she believes that it should be affordable. That's why she's been fighting day in and day out to protect these fundamental rights -- and to make sure that no one ends up with a surprise medical bill in the process.
[**Senator Hassan needs to know that you 're with her in this fight, so will you please add your name to our petition and call on President Trump to stop attacking health care?**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc2f9/2a380d31/38442a17/f12e92/1245523495/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "https://petitions.signforgood.com/protect-the-ACA/?code=Hassan&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2F2019_protect-the-aca-up%3Frefcode%3De20190417_acaja_all")
Thank you for speaking up,
Team Maggie

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: A Real Attorney General
body:
“Washington pile-ons are never pretty, but this week’s political setup of Attorney General William Barr is disreputable even by Beltway standards.” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cf43b9bdbd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **A Real Attorney General**

“Washington pile-ons are never pretty, but this week’s political setup of Attorney General William Barr is disreputable even by Beltway standards,” _The Wall Street Journal_ editorial board writes.

“Democrats and the media are turning the AG into a villain for doing his duty and making the hard decisions that special counsel Robert Mueller abdicated . . . This trashing of Bill Barr shows how frustrated and angry Democrats continue to be that the special counsel came up empty in his Russia collusion probe.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2682f30268&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Democrats spent Wednesday’s Senate Judiciary Committee hearing trying to pin some serious wrongdoing on Attorney General William Barr, or at least on President Trump — and completely struck out,” the _[New York Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fa475e5bb1&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “As the AG calmly noted, Mueller never claimed Barr’s memo was ‘inaccurate or misleading,’ only that ‘press reporting’ on it was.”
---
|
---
| “A Las Vegas federal grand jury returned a three-count indictment [yesterday] against three alleged members of La Mara Salvatrucha (MS-13) gang for their involvement in a kidnapping and murder,” [KSNV Las Vegas](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=55a890ed0b&e=3ee4985eea) reports. Two of the three men charged are citizens of El Salvador, and the third is a citizen of Honduras. “All three are in the United States illegally.”
---
|
---
| Congressional Democrats grossly misled Americans about tax cuts, and the mainstream media failed to hold them accountable for it, Scott Jennings writes in the _[Courier-Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4e403ddaae&e=3ee4985eea)_. “According to an NBC News/Wall Street Journal poll, just 17% of Americans believed their taxes went down. What’s the truth? Let’s ask The New York Times, which — after printing lie after lie regarding the tax cut — finally reported in April: ‘If you’re an American taxpayer, you probably got a tax cut last year.’”
---
|
---
| “The U.S. Commerce Department’s blockbuster news, that the economy grew by a stunning 3.2 percent in the first quarter, is good news for everybody . . . A little bipartisan celebration of the news is in order,” _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e71079b1ed&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “Tax cuts, deregulation and renegotiated trade is working. This is all the more remarkable given how many ‘experts’ predicted economic catastrophe on the morning after the 2016 election.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3078a33536&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8537d26615&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=9573e68bdb)
---

---

This legislation session, our state leaders were hard at work. They passed an honestly-balanced budget (with responsible reserves), boosted... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/governor-holcomb-signs-pro-life-legislation?e=1acd9bb3d6)

| This legislative session, our state leaders were hard at work. They passed an honestly-balanced budget (with responsible reserves), boosted education funding, helped skill-up our workforce, attacked the opioid crisis and more.

But they didn't stop there. They also took decisive action to protect the sanctity of life.

_**Because of the action of the Indiana General Assembly and Governor Eric Holcomb's signature, unborn Hoosiers are now protected from the tragedy of dismemberment abortions.**_

Additionally, with Governor Holcomb's signature, another new state law says that nurses, pharmacists and physician assistants can refuse to participate in abortions, extending to them a right already enjoyed by doctors, health clinic staffers and other types of hospital employees in Indiana.
**[\-- > THANK GOVERNOR HOLCOMB & OUR LEGISLATORS: DONATE NOW <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=96d41f1f2f&e=1acd9bb3d6)**
These are _**MAJOR pro-life wins for Hoosiers**_ , and have earned accolades from groups including Indiana Right to Life ([read what they're saying here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=305e516fe2&e=1acd9bb3d6))!

_**And, of course, Governor Holcomb is continuing his work to make Indiana home to the lowest infant mortality rate in the Midwest by 2024.**_

This session, Governor Holcomb championed legislation that is connecting more women to prenatal and postnatal care, and identifies substance abuse disorder earlier in pregnancy, keeping both the mother and baby healthy and happy. And this continues his work composing standards of care for birthing facilities across Indiana.

This is critical work. And know that with Governor Holcomb and our Republican leaders in Indiana, the sanctity of life will be protected!

To thank Governor Holcomb and our legislative leaders, please consider [sharing this post on Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a58895453f&e=1acd9bb3d6), [this post on Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=03e39d08f5&e=1acd9bb3d6), or [making a donation to our efforts](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d2454e1254&e=1acd9bb3d6) at the Indiana Republican Party to help elect pro-life candidates.

name: Kirsten (personal)
subject: I can't say it enough--thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand 2020
body:
| | |
---
Hi Peter,
Everywhere I go, I'm struck by how generous everyone is--with your support, your time and your stories. **Thank you** for everything you've done for our campaign so far.
I spent last weekend in New Hampshire, and I wanted to take a second to make sure you had a chance to see what I did in the Granite State--because it's only thanks to supporters like you that I was able to go.
**Every donation to this campaign helps keep me out on the road, hearing from voters and building support.** And that's how we're going to win this campaign: one voter, one coffee shop, one town hall, one meeting at a time.
Take a look at a few of my favorite photos from New Hampshire:
_This is Scout! She runs a lemonade stand to help children at the border --she is what bold and brave leadership looks like._
_My friend and former candidate for NH governor Molly Kelly moderated our town hall at Keene State. We had an important conversation about how to combat sexual assault on college campuses, close the wealth gap and more._
_Health care was on the minds of the people attending our house party in Milford. I will always fight for every family as hard as I fight for my own --and for health care to be a right, not a privilege._
Our movement is people-powered, and I'm really glad you're in it with me.
I know I'm an underdog right now. But I'm used to that. And I'm so proud of the campaign we're running--one based on big, bold, brave ideas and solutions to our country's problems. I have every faith that together, we can prove the naysayers wrong.
Thank you for everything,

Kirsten
| | To guarantee her spot on the Democratic debate stage, Kirsten needs support from 65,000 individual Americans. name: Andrew Yang
subject: Trust the Process
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang
body:
| | Friend --
[](http://my.yang2020.com/?e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=1) Hello and thank you for your support! It means a ton to me and the team. I’m writing this on the plane out West—we are having a rally in [Seattle on Friday](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghcUJkVmfetEAp2sK9sThwBXl6kF9b220zNTt1ymCeJCsZOe56OrYKLWUp764mKCE5A&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=2 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Seattle"), [Detroit on Saturday](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghePf9lMScPeEghyrDEJTI372Jo_PAP0tilgrUxF9-_kIhZdxRNpjs9OINDEUSjPWdOqhtCqG3Dl8BRJiUq-EIXY&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=3 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in
Detroit"), and [Minneapolis on Sunday](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=uuUyPGiVHWR_V3cUbcvCWdikqgOEmv95as43Y58cghfQNMwNimpI6IcIFBC904TiQMcwbKUEVi49gZIY2_StcdgI8QQPhoX7uuq0QjXCUcw&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=4 "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in
Minneapolis"), and are stopping in the Bay Area for a day first to say hello to some people there. Someone asked the other day whether it’s me writing these emails. Yep. It’s me. I tend to write these emails in transit from one place to another. I enjoy processing what’s happening in the campaign. I find it a useful way to recharge. I was talking to my wife last night about introverts who are/were politicians. It’s probably not the norm. Having met Barack Obama several times, I believe that he qualifies. After meeting him, my wife said, “For an introvert, he seemed very personable.” I was a very nerdy, bookish kid. My favorite thing to do was read books. As a kid, I devoured biographies of Native American leaders and fantasy books: Dragonlance, Piers Anthony, Terry Brooks, Anne McCaffrey. I played _Dungeons & Dragons_ with my brother and our neighbors growing up, and later moved on to video games. I struggled with various social interactions throughout my adolescence, particularly when people started drinking. I went from nerdy kid to somewhat mopey adolescent, listening to the Smiths and the Cure. I was in the high school play and joined the debate team. But I was still the last person that anyone thought would become a politician much less run for President. My high school yearbook [photo recently appeared in _New York Magazine_](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=bcBHfmaHyz4Lgng7HUtaWLzU86hdyRg3EOtw3vq622fEgPCr0Y-HlkNK6nfCkz-0GpIE7xxb2c3X0al5Xj98pVR2KWUktc3hi5BTn6fqC0XZnc9TWuBaaqF0xF5U2MNtJgXzcai_Yg5nKEpFsbRFhg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=5 "The Yearbook Photos of the 2020 Presidential
Candidates"). I suppose my fashion choices are proof that I’d never intended to run for anything. I had a bit of a chip on my shoulder, and started working out and playing basketball in my late-teens. Going to the gym became another way to process things. I noticed recently that my emails have become a bit similar. They say something like, “Look how much awesome progress we are making! Here are some news clippings and photos! We are going to grow and grow and shock the world!” All of which is real and sincere. I mean, it’s staggering how far we’ve come. Now that we are cool, I realize just how uncool we were until recently. #YangGang. I appreciate all of the creativity and artwork that people are producing—including the good memes. People being excited enough about this campaign to put their time and energy into it is awesome. Thank you for believing in me. But there will be more to this campaign and my speaking to you than simply cataloging our recent achievements. My recent emails have fallen into what I’d consider “Executive Mode.” Building an organization—or a presidential campaign—includes advancing certain variables and deliverables. As an executive, you focus on them and try to move them forward. Let’s say you were running for President: How do you become known to more Americans? Let’s do some press interviews, starting with podcasts. How do you become politically viable? Let’s make 11 trips each to New Hampshire and Iowa and start schmoozing with Democratic activists there. How do you raise millions of dollars to fund a campaign? Let’s put a vision out to our fellow Americans that gets people excited about building a movement for a new human-centered economy. How do you build a massive following? [Hold big and exciting rallies around the country](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=6 "Humanity First Tour Schedule") for thousands of supporters. The press will see the crowds and respond. Making progress on these variables requires a constant array of distinct behaviors. E.g., yesterday, I woke up, went to a TV studio, put makeup and hairspray on, and had a 6-minute conversation on a cable news program. Then I filmed various messages for online ads. Then I did a Town Hall. My communications become a summary of these activities. “We did this, and then we did this and we made progress. Isn’t it great?” This is a written form of executive communication. I will do my fair share of it—it is my job—but I promise to go beyond it to give you some genuine insight into what running for President actually entails and what the process is. It will be better for me too. As an adult, I remained an introvert, but I found myself developing different capacities. Mainly because I revolted when I found out it was supposed to be my job to process other people’s documentation as a corporate attorney. I was like, “No way this is what I’m going to do for a living. I need to figure something else out.” I was willing to adopt behaviors that were very uncomfortable for me at the time to reach certain goals. Putting myself out there. Selling an idea for a company. Asking women out on dates. (I am now grateful to be married.) Over time, those behaviors became, if not second nature, at least things I could do in service of something I felt strongly about. I even started throwing parties in my late twenties. It was a way to socialize and be cool when I didn’t have an apartment to host anything (I wasn’t making much money). I remember distinctly walking home alone, late at night, stone cold sober after a big party that had included hundreds of people. Throwing parties was perhaps the best training for a presidential campaign. Years later, my organization Venture for America had an annual gala that drew hundreds of people. I’d give a speech each year. Starting and running a national non-profit involved a lot of speaking and presenting a vision. I joked that I felt like a priest, where my job was to show up and bless things. This is, I believe, how an introvert finds himself running for President. You have certain goals. You realize that the only way to make them happen is certain behaviors. You get better at those behaviors. Eventually, you find yourself with a goal so big that it is almost impossible—prepare society for the automation of labor that is already tearing us apart. Keep the country whole. There is a vanishingly short list of ways to meaningfully do so. I’ve said in various forums that I don’t much care for the actual job of being a politician. It’s true. I think one of the reasons we despair for our future is that politicians traffic in certain behaviors and get rewarded for them. There is little need for deep thought or consideration. We lurch from press release to press release, from cable news appearance to cable news appearance, from speech to speech, with the constant buzz of our approval and popularity in the background. And money is tied to that approval. At first, I was horrified that how many social media follows I got was now a key deliverable. I was like, “I’m a grown man now evaluating myself like an eighth-grader.” (_Eighth Grade_ was a good movie.) But I adapted. Because I have a job to do. We all do. I will do what it takes to get this job done. We are off to a tremendous start thanks to all of you. We have the capacity to change the course of our country for the better. But I need your help. The title of this email was taken from a slogan adopted by the fans of an NBA Team, the 76ers, who had a highly introverted GM for a number of years, Sam Hinkie. He wrote [a resignation letter](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=5ZLRwb4uUn5Hg-Y6Zv1G__U45BFvbtaSAjN4PCeSAeL1bdkUhToTZ-nTmuJ1wtPjYidgpH6nqFJHlxBy8S8BNg&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=7 "Sam Hinkie's
Resignation Letter") that became something of a cult hit. Many of his ideas have since been adopted by other teams. This campaign is going to be a triumph of big ideas and solutions over the churn of politics as usual. There is an enormous appetite for a different kind of movement. Let’s provide it. It’s not Left or Right, it’s Forward. Let’s do the job that history has left for us. Do something that makes you uncomfortable today. It is part of the process. And be sure to [come to a rally if we're hosting one near you](https://my.yang2020.com/r?u=c5dAo5qulKAncvNYjq2uGcnHAN92nM1CHInwgA-PIi7HdTRyEez5JIs0AF2zI3zA&e=d12a2bba9049b8d4852b6e0020f599b5&utm_source=yang2020&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=050219_newsletter_v2&n=8 "Humanity First Tour Schedule"). Energized by you all, \- Andrew
---

clean_body: | | Friend -- Hello and thank you for your support! It means a ton to me and the team. I’m writing this on the plane out West—we are having a rally in "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Seattle", "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Detroit", and "Andrew Yang's Humanity First Rally in Minneapolis", and are stopping in the Bay Area for a day first to say hello to some people there. Someone asked the other day whether it’s me writing these emails. Yep. It’s me. I tend to write these emails in transit from one place to another. I enjoy processing what’s happening in the campaign. I find it a useful way to recharge. I was talking to my wife last night about introverts who are/were politicians. It’s probably not the norm. Having met Barack Obama several times, I believe that he qualifies. After meeting him, my wife said, “For an introvert, he seemed very personable.” I was a very nerdy, bookish kid. My favorite thing to do was read books. As a kid, I devoured biographies of Native American leaders and fantasy books: Dragonlance, Piers Anthony, Terry Brooks, Anne McCaffrey. I played Dungeons & Dragons with my brother and our neighbors growing up, and later moved on to video games. I struggled with various social interactions throughout my adolescence, particularly when people started drinking. I went from nerdy kid to somewhat mopey adolescent, listening to the Smiths and the Cure. I was in the high school play and joined the debate team. But I was still the last person that anyone thought would become a politician much less run for President. My high school yearbook "The Yearbook Photos of the 2020 Presidential Candidates". I suppose my fashion choices are proof that I’d never intended to run for anything. I had a bit of a chip on my shoulder, and started working out and playing basketball in my late-teens. Going to the gym became another way to process things. I noticed recently that my emails have become a bit similar. They say something like, “Look how much awesome progress we are making! Here are some news clippings and photos! We are going to grow and grow and shock the world!” All of which is real and sincere. I mean, it’s staggering how far we’ve come. Now that we are cool, I realize just how uncool we were until recently. #YangGang. I appreciate all of the creativity and artwork that people are producing—including the good memes. People being excited enough about this campaign to put their time and energy into it is awesome. Thank you for believing in me. But there will be more to this campaign and my speaking to you than simply cataloging our recent achievements. My recent emails have fallen into what I’d consider “Executive Mode.” Building an organization—or a presidential campaign—includes advancing certain variables and deliverables. As an executive, you focus on them and try to move them forward. Let’s say you were running for President: How do you become known to more Americans? Let’s do some press interviews, starting with podcasts. How do you become politically viable? Let’s make 11 trips each to New Hampshire and Iowa and start schmoozing with Democratic activists there. How do you raise millions of dollars to fund a campaign? Let’s put a vision out to our fellow Americans that gets people excited about building a movement for a new human-centered economy. How do you build a massive following? "Humanity First Tour Schedule" for thousands of supporters. The press will see the crowds and respond. Making progress on these variables requires a constant array of distinct behaviors. E.g., yesterday, I woke up, went to a TV studio, put makeup and hairspray on, and had a 6-minute conversation on a cable news program. Then I filmed various messages for online ads. Then I did a Town Hall. My communications become a summary of these activities. “We did this, and then we did this and we made progress. Isn’t it great?” This is a written form of executive communication. I will do my fair share of it—it is my job—but I promise to go beyond it to give you some genuine insight into what running for President actually entails and what the process is. It will be better for me too. As an adult, I remained an introvert, but I found myself developing different capacities. Mainly because I revolted when I found out it was supposed to be my job to process other people’s documentation as a corporate attorney. I was like, “No way this is what I’m going to do for a living. I need to figure something else out.” I was willing to adopt behaviors that were very uncomfortable for me at the time to reach certain goals. Putting myself out there. Selling an idea for a company. Asking women out on dates. (I am now grateful to be married.) Over time, those behaviors became, if not second nature, at least things I could do in service of something I felt strongly about. I even started throwing parties in my late twenties. It was a way to socialize and be cool when I didn’t have an apartment to host anything (I wasn’t making much money). I remember distinctly walking home alone, late at night, stone cold sober after a big party that had included hundreds of people. Throwing parties was perhaps the best training for a presidential campaign. Years later, my organization Venture for America had an annual gala that drew hundreds of people. I’d give a speech each year. Starting and running a national non-profit involved a lot of speaking and presenting a vision. I joked that I felt like a priest, where my job was to show up and bless things. This is, I believe, how an introvert finds himself running for President. You have certain goals. You realize that the only way to make them happen is certain behaviors. You get better at those behaviors. Eventually, you find yourself with a goal so big that it is almost impossible—prepare society for the automation of labor that is already tearing us apart. Keep the country whole. There is a vanishingly short list of ways to meaningfully do so. I’ve said in various forums that I don’t much care for the actual job of being a politician. It’s true. I think one of the reasons we despair for our future is that politicians traffic in certain behaviors and get rewarded for them. There is little need for deep thought or consideration. We lurch from press release to press release, from cable news appearance to cable news appearance, from speech to speech, with the constant buzz of our approval and popularity in the background. And money is tied to that approval. At first, I was horrified that how many social media follows I got was now a key deliverable. I was like, “I’m a grown man now evaluating myself like an eighth-grader.” (Eighth Grade was a good movie.) But I adapted. Because I have a job to do. We all do. I will do what it takes to get this job done. We are off to a tremendous start thanks to all of you. We have the capacity to change the course of our country for the better. But I need your help. The title of this email was taken from a slogan adopted by the fans of an NBA Team, the 76ers, who had a highly introverted GM for a number of years, Sam Hinkie. He wrote "Sam Hinkie's Resignation Letter" that became something of a cult hit. Many of his ideas have since been adopted by other teams. This campaign is going to be a triumph of big ideas and solutions over the churn of politics as usual. There is an enormous appetite for a different kind of movement. Let’s provide it. It’s not Left or Right, it’s Forward. Let’s do the job that history has left for us. Do something that makes you uncomfortable today. It is part of the process. And be sure to "Humanity First Tour Schedule". Energized by you all, \- Andrew Can you make a donation today? Most of our donations come from people like you chipping in small amounts when they can. Your continued generosity helps us invest in people. Join the Yang Gang! If you'd like to volunteer to help with grassroots organizing, please fill out an organizer application and someone from our team will reach out. | Thank you for helping to make Universal Basic Income a reality. | | | Paid for by Friends of Andrew Yang. All rights reserved. New York, NY #HumanityFirst

name: =?utf-8?Q?Mike=20Gravel=2C=20Marlon=20Ettinger?=
subject: Macron Regime Violently Represses Trade Union Demonstration in Paris
body:
| | | **The Empire Feeds on the Republic**
---
| | Yesterday was International Worker's Day, and another day in the long chronicle of the French Macron regime’s brutality. President Macron, who despite being elected by a minority of his country’s electorate has his admirers in the United States among such luminaries as Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton, and a capable cover band version in Pete Buttigieg, leads a regime that, since last November, has had a United Nations inquiry opened into its violent and vicious repression of domestic protest. 40,000 workers came out at the call of the CGT, one of France's largest trade union confederations, to rally in celebration of their hard and long-fought for rights and to continue demanding more. The Parisian authorities responded with tear gas and riot police. The Geneva Protocol, which the United States did not ratify until 1975, and only after haggling for concessions, bans chemical and germ warfare. George Wald, a Nobel laureate, quoted by Anna Feigenbaum in her illuminating history of tear gas 'Tear Gas: From the Battlefields of World War I to the Streets of Today,' contended that "under combat conditions, tear gas is part of a thoroughly lethal operation." As the Gilet Jaunes protest movement approaches half a year of violent repression by the Macron regime, we should look at this escalating application of violent state power. France's militarism and imperialist domination of the Sahel has continued unabated in the decades since the liberation of its colonies (which are still dominated by French capital). **_Militarism abroad interacts with militarism at home. It’s no surprise that the most nakedly authoritarian fascist thugs have come to power in the United States after more than three decades of unalloyed veneration of the military and its conquests in mainstream American culture._** [Make a $1.00 donation to help Mike stand with workers](https://mikegravel.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4840f01d672bb468321c6d3b8&id=78a82bebd7&e=85f7472d66) It also isn’t surprising that in France, after fourteen lost eyes, a slew of lost hands, and at least 150 grave injuries, chemical warfare is once again used against workers demonstrating for their rights as human beings. War is worth being condemned on its own, but also for the fact that war always comes home. The massive levels of violence in the U.S. are no coincidence given the actions of its military abroad. I was in the riots in Paris in November, and burned by the tear gas that the police fired wantonly into crowds of protestors. Tear gas burns and chokes, but its effects do go away. Now I don’t feel the chest-seizing choking that tear gas hits you with, or the raking burning feeling in the eyes it produces. The same can’t be said for the eyeless of France, the handless who protested to assert their rights as citizens in a democracy. And neither it can’t be said for the victims of France and America’s wars of imperial conquest. It’s all got to stop. In Solidarity with the workers in France and around the world fighting to end war and exploitation, Marlon J. Ettinger
National Press Secretary, Gravel 2020
[marlon@mikegravel.org](mailto:marlon@mikegravel.org)

George Wald, a Nobel laureate, quoted by Anna Feigenbaum in her illuminating history of tear gas 'Tear Gas: From the Battlefields of World War I to the Streets of Today,' contended that "under combat conditions, tear gas is part of a thoroughly lethal operation." As the Gilet Jaunes protest movement approaches half a year of violent repression by the Macron regime, we should look at this escalating application of violent state power. France's militarism and imperialist domination of the Sahel has continued unabated in the decades since the liberation of its colonies (which are still dominated by French capital). Militarism abroad interacts with militarism at home. It’s no surprise that the most nakedly authoritarian fascist thugs have come to power in the United States after more than three decades of unalloyed veneration of the military and its conquests in mainstream American culture. It also isn’t surprising that in France, after fourteen lost eyes, a slew of lost hands, and at least 150 grave injuries, chemical warfare is once again used against workers demonstrating for their rights as human beings. War is worth being condemned on its own, but also for the fact that war always comes home. The massive levels of violence in the U.S. are no coincidence given the actions of its military abroad. I was in the riots in Paris in November, and burned by the tear gas that the police fired wantonly into crowds of protestors. Tear gas burns and chokes, but its effects do go away. Now I don’t feel the chest-seizing choking that tear gas hits you with, or the raking burning feeling in the eyes it produces. The same can’t be said for the eyeless of France, the handless who protested to assert their rights as citizens in a democracy. And neither it can’t be said for the victims of France and America’s wars of imperial conquest. It’s all got to stop. In Solidarity with the workers in France and around the world fighting to end war and exploitation, Marlon J. Attorney General William Barr's conduct before the Senate, his handling of the Mueller report, and his refusal to testify before the House of Representatives shows his contempt. Contempt not only for Congress, but for the American people and the truth.

It's clear that Attorney General Barr must resign immediately.

If AG Barr wants to serve as President Trump's personal lawyer, he's free to do so, but he cannot be America's top law enforcement officer at the same time.
**Sign the petition and demand Attorney General Barr resign immediately!**

Dear Friend –
I’m so grateful for the outpouring of support we received yesterday when I launched my campaign. We hit over a quarter million dollars raised in our first twelve hours - $265,000 to be exact. Even more exciting, over 1,000 people joined with us as #FoundingDonors. Folks, it’s crystal clear that the enthusiasm and belief that we can win Georgia have inspired people across our state and our country. I hope you’re feeling it too.
Our campaign was featured in the Columbus Ledger-Enquirer , The New York Times , US News , CNBC , Vox and the Atlanta Journal-Constitution, and many more. Now we’re full steam ahead running a campaign to win – and making sure Georgia leads the way forward for our country.
America needs better leadership and Georgia needs better representation. Government should be a tool for creating opportunity and a framework for prosperity, not something that will hold people back and divide us. As Senator, I want to ensure that government makes a positive difference in all of our lives. The time for chaos, hate, and dysfunction is over.
It’s day two of my campaign for U.S. Senate and now the real work begins. Follow our campaign on Twitter [https://twitter.com/teresatomlinson] , Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/TeresaPikeTomlinson/] , and Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/teresapiketomlinson/] for the latest updates on the trail. We are going to work hard because the stakes couldn't be higher, but we’re going to have some fun along the way, too.
I am so honored to have you by my side as we begin this journey to 2020. Together, we will take on Washington dysfunction and make government work for the people again.
Yours truly,
Teresa

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump prays with faith leaders at the White House
body:
From the Rose Garden of the White House this morning, President Donald J. Trump joined faith leaders in prayer... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=338ff95485&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • May 2, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump prays with faith leaders at the White House**

**From the Rose Garden** of the White House this morning, President Donald J. Trump joined faith leaders in prayer and called religious liberty the bedrock of American life.

**“Every citizen** has the absolute right to live according to the teachings of their faith and the convictions of their heart,” the President said. “To protect this heritage my Administration has strongly defended religious liberty.”
🎬 **Watch:**[President Trump says we are “one Nation under God”](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4278368715&e=3ee4985eea)
**The President** has stood by communities of faith since his first day in office. Some of the gestures are small but powerful: “It's all Merry Christmas again,” he said today, referring to how many stores and politicians used to tiptoe around the Christian holiday’s name. Other actions are policy-specific. “President Trump has taken steps to ensure that the Federal Government will never, ever penalize anyone for their religious beliefs ever again,” [Vice President Mike Pence](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=78b8a7a9d3&e=3ee4985eea) said while kicking off this morning’s events.

**Today, the Trump Administration** continued that trend by [unveiling additional protections](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b86b216916&e=3ee4985eea) for medical providers to keep them from being forced to perform procedures—including abortions—that violate their faith.

**One of the most powerful moments** from today’s National Day of Prayer came when Rabbi Yisroel Goldstein took the podium. After being wounded in the shooting at the Chabad of Poway on Saturday, Rabbi Goldstein wrapped his bloodied hand in a prayer shawl, stood on a chair, and addressed the congregation: “We are a Jewish nation that will stand tall. We will not let anyone take us down.”

[Watch Rabbi Goldstein say a few words after the recent shooting](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6cdfaeecde&e=3ee4985eea).

**In photos:** [First Lady Melania Trump at last night’s prayer dinner ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2ecff8ba78&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump’s 100 th judge—confirmed! **

**President Trump** hit another milestone today, as the United States Senate confirmed his 100th judge to the Federal bench.

**These judges** join the President’s two Supreme Court appointments—Justices Neil Gorsuch and Brett Kavanaugh—in reshaping America’s judiciary after years of activist rulings. They will succeed in upholding our Nation’s founding principles for one reason: They will follow the Constitution as it is written, not as they imagine it should be.

[Something to share: 100 Trump judges protecting your Constitutional rights](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=646530498d&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **America in lockstep with the Venezuelan people**

**“I'd like to begin** by sending our prayers to the people of Venezuela in their righteous struggle for freedom,” President Trump said at today’s prayer service at the White House.

**“The brutal repression** of the Venezuelan people must end and it must end soon. People are starving. They have no food. They have no water. And this was once one of the wealthiest countries in the world . . . We will be there to help.”

**Dictator Nicolas Maduro** is following Cuba’s playbook of torture and repression in a desperate attempt to keep the people of Venezuela from hearing Interim President Juan Guaido’s message. President Trump has made it clear that any outside interference to prop up Maduro, including from Cuba and Russia, is unacceptable.

**The United States** calls on the Venezuelan military and security forces to protect all Venezuelans and accept Interim President Guaido’s legitimacy and offer of amnesty. The Trump Administration will hold accountable all those who threaten the safety of the Venezuelan people—or the restoration of Venezuelan democracy.

[President Trump addresses the situation in Venezuela](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=00911f705c&e=3ee4985eea).

**More:** [Rep. Ilhan Omar (D-MN) accuses America of “bullying” dictator Maduro](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=74c3c679fa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day

|
